target_scopes: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user withdraw, refund, execute, redirect, unlock, underpay, over-credit, or otherwise extract current live BNB, USDT, or bridge-held value from the deployed BridgeIndex proxy or dependencies beyond valid entitlement

Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim a real bridge fee, refund, withdrawal, gas reimbursement, or other payout liability that is backed by a current nonzero live asset balance
live_context_hint: BSC proxy 0x3cd6fb6b0cddd3610f0f4769aa7bb686cd4a4b55, implementation 0x1d85a38d4a3ea8a6ae74f185a1df267704e7a748, current proxy BNB and USDT balances, deposit/withdrawal/refund/execution records, resourceId-token mappings, role membership, blacklist/pause state, bot gas configuration, Bridge/voteRef/serverAddress dependencies, and event-derived live liabilities
protocol_focus: BridgeIndex is a BSC bridge contract system behind a proxy. The relevant value paths are deposits, bot-gas deposits, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, pause/blacklist checks, and role-gated bridge operations. This campaign only pays for concrete extraction of current live balances or backed bridge liabilities.
attacker_profile: unprivileged depositor, withdrawal requester, refund claimant, recipient, receiver contract, bridge user, token user, or external caller interacting through public BridgeIndex entrypoints
core_invariants: attackers must not extract proxy-held BNB, USDT, bridge fees, refunds, withdrawals, reimbursements, or mapped token value beyond valid entitlement
every deposit, withdrawal, refund, and execute path must reconcile against current live proxy balances and recorded liabilities
resourceId-token mappings, bridge pause flags, token pause flags, blacklist checks, role checks, and nonce/accounting state must not be bypassable by alternate entrypoints or malformed calldata
bot gas accounting, min fee logic, min/max amount checks, and refund math must not let an attacker receive excess value or avoid required payment
admin, finance, relayer, and bridge role functions matter only if an unprivileged path can bypass or prepare unauthorized value movement without key compromise
external token behavior or bridge dependency behavior only counts when BridgeIndex itself accepts attacker-controlled sequencing or data that causes an overpayment or unauthorized transfer
high_value_surfaces: payable deposit paths: deposit, depositWithBotGas, ReceivedEth, DepositEvent
withdrawal paths: withdrawalCreate, withdrawalApprove, execute, markExecutionConfirmed, CreatedWithdrawal, ApprovedWithdrawal, ExecuteEvent, ExecutedWithdrawal
refund paths: adminRefund, Refunded, refundDatas, fee and bot-gas reimbursement accounting
token/resource configuration: SetTokenEvent, getTokenPauseByResourceId, token address/resourceId mappings, token pause flags
role and policy controls: ADMIN_ROLE, BRIDGE_ROLE, FINANCE_ROLE, RELAYER_ROLE, hasRole, grantRole, revokeRole, blacklist, getBridgePause
dependency addresses: Bridge, voteRef, serverAddress, proxy admin, implementation, token contracts, and any event-derived bridge asset holders
math and state ordering around amount, fee, destination chain id, localNonce, execution confirmation, and refund status
impact_mapping: Fund extraction: attacker receives BNB, USDT, mapped token funds, refunds, withdrawals, bridge fees, or reimbursements without the required deposit, authorization, or entitlement
Reward extraction: attacker overclaims a real fee/refund/reimbursement/withdrawal payout that is backed by current live BridgeIndex balances
Insolvency with extraction: attacker creates or uses a bridge accounting mismatch then withdraws, refunds, executes, or claims more value than funded
Unauthorized redirection: funds or backed liabilities move to an attacker-controlled recipient, token, bridge, server, vote reference, or dependency without valid authorization
Accounting corruption only counts if it leads to attacker-controlled over-withdrawal, over-refund, over-execution, over-crediting, or backed payout overclaim
known_rejection_memory: Reject DoS, pause, blacklist, liveness, stuck transaction, gas-only, griefing, and temporary lock claims unless the same path transfers excess live value to the attacker.

Reject admin-only, finance-only, bridge-role-only, relayer-only, proxy-admin compromise, governance/key compromise, or maintainer configuration mistakes. A current live balance does not make privileged extraction reportable unless an unprivileged attacker can bypass that privilege boundary and trigger the value movement.
Reject generic reward-pool claims unless a current bridge fee, refund, withdrawal, or reimbursement liability is backed by a nonzero live asset ledger row.
Reject future-deposit-only claims, future bridge-liquidity assumptions, imagined TVL, and any candidate that cannot name the current live BNB/USDT/liability ledger row it extracts.
Reject source-only theories that do not connect to the live proxy address, implementation ABI, reachable entrypoint, concrete live value movement, and local proof plan.
